header.title: Error Boundary
header.description: UI error isolation and application stability enforcement.

benefit_text: Failure isolation allows the app to continue functioning even if a specific widget or feature crashes.

isolation.label: Crash Isolation:
isolation.text: Widgets, external components, complex UI blocks, <one>Lazy-loaded</one> components, and integrations (chats, maps).

boundaries.label: Layer Boundaries:
boundaries.text: <one>Page-level</one> (full page) and <two>Feature-level</two> (specific block: cart, chat, analytics).

dynamic.label: Dynamic Code:
dynamic.text: When code might be unstable or third-party modules are integrated.

usage.title: HOC Usage Example
usage.description: A practical example of isolating a feature using <one>withErrorBoundary</one>. Notice how the <two>HOC</two> prevents the "white screen of death" when errors occur in unstable rendering logic of child components.

architecture.title: Architecture (Best Practice)
architecture.description: Use multiple levels of protection. Don't use a single global <one>boundary</one> — you'll lose control over granular failures.
